cases.description: Writable mount can create, fsync, read, unmount, and expose data remotely.
CLI writes are visible through FUSE and FUSE writes are visible through CLI/API.
Small, large, overwrite, truncate, sparse, EOF, and checksum semantics.
mkdir, readdir, stat, chmod, rename, unlink, and rmdir.
symlink, hardlink, readlink, nlink, and target content checks.
Read-only mount allows reads and rejects mutations.
Mounting a remote subtree exposes the subtree as the mount root.
SQLite WAL workload, checkpoint, close, remount, and row verification.
Self-contained local fixture repo with drive9 git fast clone, blobless hydrate, status, edit, add, and commit.
Kill a foreground mount after writes, force detach if needed, remount with the same cache root, and verify recovery.
